hypothesis: Incorporating a chemistry-grounded valency-aromaticity consistency loss, derived from refined narom/vother aromatic-bond accounting and Kekulization-aware valency tables, into the objective of diffusion-based 3D molecular generators will reduce valency violations by at least 30% and improve GFN2-xTB–based geometry metrics (bond-length/angle deviations and ∆Erelax) by at least 15% on GEOM-Drugs and diverse held-out chemistries, compared to models trained without this loss.
